records.ety: [OE. bihalden, biholden, AS. behealdan to hold, have in sight; pref. be- + healdan to hold, keep; akin to G. behalten to hold, keep. See Hold.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Beheld (<?/) (p. p. formerly Beholden (<?/), now used only as a p. a.); p. pr. & vb. n. Beholding.]

defs: To have in sight; to see clearly; to look at; to regard with the eyes.
quotes: When he beheld the serpent of brass, he lived.
Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world.

defs: To direct the eyes to, or fix them upon, an object; to look; to see.
quotes: And I beheld, and, lo, in the midst of the throne, . . . a lamb as it had been slain.
